decisions.title: Why Scan-and-Claim over Assignment
decisions.description: Instead of a central coordinator assigning tasks, each autonomous agent scans a shared task board and claims tasks it can handle. This eliminates the single point of failure and bottleneck of a central dispatcher. If one agent goes down, others continue claiming and completing work. The pattern mirrors how open-source contributors pick issues from a tracker.
decisions.alternatives: Central assignment (a manager agent dispatches tasks) is easier to reason about and avoids duplicate work. However, it creates a single point of failure and a bottleneck. Pull-based systems like scan-and-claim scale better and are more resilient. Duplicate claims are resolved with atomic compare-and-swap on the task file.

zh.title: 为什么用扫描认领而非集中分配
zh.description: 不使用中央协调者分配任务，而是每个自主 Agent 扫描共享任务板并认领自己能处理的任务。这消除了中央调度器的单点故障和瓶颈。如果一个 Agent 宕机，其他 Agent 继续认领和完成工作。这个模式类似于开源贡献者从 issue 跟踪器中挑选任务。

decisions.title: Why Timeout-Based Self-Governance
decisions.description: Each claimed task has a timeout. If the claiming agent does not complete the task within the timeout, the task returns to the available pool for other agents to claim. This handles agent crashes, infinite loops, and stuck states without requiring external monitoring. The system is self-healing: no human needs to notice and intervene when an agent fails.
decisions.alternatives: Heartbeat-based monitoring (agents periodically report they are alive) detects failures faster but requires additional infrastructure. Manual intervention is reliable but does not scale. Timeout-based governance is the simplest self-healing mechanism that requires zero additional components.

zh.title: 为什么使用基于超时的自治管理
zh.description: 每个被认领的任务都有超时限制。如果认领的 Agent 未能在超时前完成任务，该任务回到可用池供其他 Agent 认领。这无需外部监控就能处理 Agent 崩溃、无限循环和卡死状态。系统是自愈的：当 Agent 失败时，不需要人类注意到并介入。
